title: The ANTI-Social Network

jokes.text: They blew on their sign until it came off 'cause they thought it was cool?
jokes.setup: Discussion about a missing sign and whether owners removed it purposely
jokes.punchline: Character misinterprets 'blow down' literally as someone physically blowing air
jokes.explanation: Perfect character stupidity - the literal interpretation of 'blow down' shows classic Always Sunny character logic

dialogue.line: They blew on their sign until it came off 'cause they thought it was cool?

jokes.text: He and his Jews have connected the whole world, and now they're toppling regimes.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Facebook and Bill Gates connecting the world
jokes.punchline: Casual antisemitic conspiracy theory delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Dark humor typical of Charlie's conspiracy mindset, though relatively low-hanging fruit for the show

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He and his Jews have connected the whole world, and now they're toppling regimes.

jokes.text: And Egypt and Japan and the Jews are all peaceful together...
jokes.setup: Continuation of Charlie's Facebook conspiracy theory
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical grouping of countries and 'the Jews' as if they're a geographical location
jokes.explanation: Charlie's distinctive brand of confused geography mixed with casual prejudice

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: And Egypt and Japan and the Jews are all peaceful together...

jokes.text: Do you think a human being can blow a sign off of a building? I didn't until Mac said it.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to clarify he never said someone could blow a sign off
jokes.punchline: Charlie's innocent admission that he believed it because Mac supposedly said it
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Charlie's gullibility and Mac's frustration with being misquoted

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Do you think a human being can blow a sign off of a building? I didn't until Mac said it.

jokes.text: In Charlie's defense, you could loosen the screws, get up there and blow...
jokes.setup: Mac insisting humans can't blow signs off buildings
jokes.punchline: Frank trying to find a technical loophole to make the absurd scenario work
jokes.explanation: Frank's practical approach to an absurd problem is quintessentially his character

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: In Charlie's defense, you could loosen the screws, get up there and blow...

jokes.text: Ugh. Ew. What the hell was that?
jokes.setup: Someone makes a disgusted reaction sound
jokes.punchline: Dee's immediate horrified reaction to whatever just happened
jokes.explanation: Basic gross-out reaction, functional but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Ugh. Ew. What the hell was that?

jokes.text: We'll do a really cool video of how we serve gin in spaghetti cans or something like that.
jokes.setup: Gang discussing making a viral video for Paddy's
jokes.punchline: Frank's bizarre idea to serve gin in spaghetti cans as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Frank idea that's both disgusting and somehow logical to him

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: We'll do a really cool video of how we serve gin in spaghetti cans or something like that.

jokes.text: I got the sign down. And there's no way a man could have blown that off of our bar.
jokes.setup: Ongoing debate about whether humans can blow signs down
jokes.punchline: Mac still insisting it's impossible while announcing he took their sign down
jokes.explanation: Mac's stubborn adherence to being right while contradicting himself

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I got the sign down. And there's no way a man could have blown that off of our bar.

jokes.text: I never once said that!
jokes.setup: Charlie and Frank saying Mac claimed people could blow signs off
jokes.punchline: Mac's increasingly frustrated denial of something he never said
jokes.explanation: Mac's exasperation at being misquoted is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I never once said that!

jokes.text: The guy that shushed us last night-- is that bugging anybody else?
jokes.setup: Gang trying to focus on important business matters
jokes.punchline: Dennis completely derailing to obsess over being shushed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism - can't let go of a minor slight to his ego

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The guy that shushed us last night-- is that bugging anybody else?

jokes.text: I don't shush them like they're animals. I use my words.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why being shushed bothered him
jokes.punchline: Dennis's pretentious distinction between shushing and telling people to be quiet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dennis character work - his narcissistic need to feel superior even in how he silences people

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I don't shush them like they're animals. I use my words.

jokes.text: And we'll pop a shush on the guy and we'll be right back.
jokes.setup: Dennis getting obsessed with confronting the shusher
jokes.punchline: Dennis treating 'shushing back' like a military operation with casual violence language
jokes.explanation: Dennis's casual aggression and treating minor slights as major offenses

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: And we'll pop a shush on the guy and we'll be right back.

jokes.text: Facebook. Found him.
jokes.setup: Dennis saying it's impossible to find someone on Facebook without knowing their name
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately and impossibly finding the exact person
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - the impossibility happening immediately after being declared impossible

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Facebook. Found him.

jokes.text: What if you hit it faster?
jokes.setup: Waiting for someone to respond to a friend request
jokes.punchline: Charlie's belief that refreshing faster will make someone respond quicker
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie technology confusion and magical thinking

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What if you hit it faster?

jokes.text: for all this guy knows, I'm a psychopath with a trunk full of chainsaws, right?
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why the shusher was rude
jokes.punchline: Dennis's casual reference to being a potential chainsaw-wielding psychopath
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dennis character moment - his casual references to violence reveal his true nature

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: for all this guy knows, I'm a psychopath with a trunk full of chainsaws, right?

jokes.text: And I could be a man with a fist full of hammers... and a trunk full of duct tape and zip ties.
jokes.setup: Dennis talking about being dangerous
jokes.punchline: Charlie escalating with specific serial killer supplies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dennis's threat with Charlie's distinctive specificity about violence

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: And I could be a man with a fist full of hammers... and a trunk full of duct tape and zip ties.

jokes.text: Yeah, people who could have chainsaws. Or hammers.
jokes.setup: Explaining to bartender about the rude shusher
jokes.punchline: Casually bringing up their earlier psychopath conversation to a stranger
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how they treat their violent fantasies as normal conversation

dialogue.line: Yeah, people who could have chainsaws. Or hammers.

jokes.text: Don't think I'd care.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking bartender how he'd feel about being shushed
jokes.punchline: Bartender's completely reasonable, calm response to their obsession
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response highlighting how ridiculous their obsession is

dialogue.speaker: Bartender
dialogue.line: Don't think I'd care.

jokes.text: Shh!
jokes.setup: Bartender saying he wouldn't care about being shushed
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately shushing him to prove his point
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and irony - Dennis becoming the very thing he hates to prove his point

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Shh!

jokes.text: I mean, I was finished speaking.
jokes.setup: Dennis shushing the bartender to show how annoying it is
jokes.punchline: Bartender pointing out the shush didn't actually interrupt him
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Dennis's dramatic demonstration with pure logic

dialogue.speaker: Bartender
dialogue.line: I mean, I was finished speaking.

jokes.text: It's like an online shush.
jokes.setup: Waiting for someone to accept their friend request
jokes.punchline: Mac interpreting lack of response as digital rudeness equivalent to shushing
jokes.explanation: Clever observation connecting their obsession to modern social media behavior

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's like an online shush.

jokes.text: Say, 'I got infected.' Get that away from me.
jokes.setup: Frank filming what he thinks is a viral video
jokes.punchline: Mac's genuine disgust reaction becomes part of Frank's confused video concept
jokes.explanation: Sets up Frank's misunderstanding of viral vs virus concepts

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Say, 'I got infected.'

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Get that away from me.

jokes.text: I am a man-whore. I hate women. I am a liar.
jokes.setup: Reading Dylan's Facebook updates
jokes.punchline: Increasingly awful self-admissions posted publicly
jokes.explanation: Basic escalating reveals, functional but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Dylan Toback
dialogue.line: I am a man-whore. I hate women. I am a liar.

jokes.text: A silk sash at Tootie's?
jokes.setup: Reading that Dylan is shopping for a silk sash
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate judgment of the pretentious accessory choice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's quick judgments about masculinity

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: A silk sash at Tootie's?

jokes.text: Daddy got a new plan.
jokes.setup: Mac getting excited about tracking down Dylan
jokes.punchline: Mac referring to himself as 'Daddy' in third person
jokes.explanation: Cringey Mac self-aggrandizement, perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Daddy got a new plan.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, yeah... He was raping us... Well... wha...? He was raping you. Yeah, well, yeah...
jokes.setup: Trying to explain why shushing was assault
jokes.punchline: Escalating to false rape claims with increasingly confused justification
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing how far they'll go to justify their obsession, very uncomfortable

dialogue.line: Yeah, well, yeah... He was raping us... Well... wha...? He was raping you. Yeah, well, yeah...

jokes.text: No. You can't rape two guys.
jokes.setup: Detective asking if both were raped
jokes.punchline: Dennis's matter-of-fact statement about rape logistics
jokes.explanation: Darkly absurd Dennis logic applied to horrible situations

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No. You can't rape two guys.

jokes.text: The guy never quite made it inside of me but, my friend here, yeah... He, like, really, kinda made soup of your insides...
jokes.setup: Continuing their false rape story
jokes.punchline: Increasingly graphic and ridiculous details about fictional assault
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable escalation that's more shocking than clever

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: The guy never quite made it inside of me but, my friend here, yeah... He, like, really, kinda made soup of your insides...

jokes.text: And the guy was super-small, so I'm fine.
jokes.setup: Charlie describing his fictional rape
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual dismissal due to the rapist's supposed size
jokes.explanation: Charlie's innocent delivery of horrible content creates uncomfortable laughs

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: And the guy was super-small, so I'm fine.

jokes.text: That's him. That is him.
jokes.setup: Spotting someone they think is Dylan at Tootie's
jokes.punchline: Their confident identification of obviously the wrong person
jokes.explanation: Setup for mistaken identity, functional but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's him.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That is him.

jokes.text: Sorry about that, ma'am. Hi, ma'am. Uh, we thought you were this guy, Dylan Toback.
jokes.setup: Approaching someone they thought was Dylan
jokes.punchline: Realizing they've mistaken a woman for a man
jokes.explanation: Basic mistaken identity payoff, gets the job done

dialogue.line: Sorry about that, ma'am. Hi, ma'am. Uh, we thought you were this guy, Dylan Toback.

jokes.text: I did because of your hair. Here's why: it's the hair and it's the cardigan, and it's the build... It's the way you stand, and the broad shoulders.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain the mistaken identity
jokes.punchline: Mac digging himself deeper by listing all the masculine qualities he saw
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of awkwardness as Mac makes it worse by explaining

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I did because of your hair. Here's why: it's the hair and it's the cardigan, and it's the build... It's the way you stand, and the broad shoulders.

jokes.text: 'Sally, send me money.' 'Sally, mail me hair.'
jokes.setup: Sally explaining what Dylan would ask for
jokes.punchline: Escalating from normal requests to bizarre hair-mailing
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd escalation - 'mail me hair' is memorably weird and unexpected

dialogue.speaker: Dylan
dialogue.line: Sally, send me money.

dialogue.speaker: Dylan
dialogue.line: Sally, mail me hair.

jokes.text: Mail him hair? Weird, right?
jokes.setup: Sally mentioning the hair-mailing request
jokes.punchline: Dennis's understated reaction to something completely insane
jokes.explanation: Good reaction that highlights the absurdity through understatement

dialogue.speaker: Sally
dialogue.line: Mail him hair? Weird, right?

jokes.text: Aw, those disgusting ex-girlfriend porno sites! I mean, there's so many of them, though! Which one?
jokes.setup: Sally mentioning naked photos posted online
jokes.punchline: Mac's transparent attempt to get the website information while pretending disgust
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac horny desperation masked as moral outrage, perfectly executed

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Aw, those disgusting ex-girlfriend porno sites! I mean, there's so many of them, though! Which one?

jokes.text: But the hair I mailed was mine. That was real.
jokes.setup: Sally explaining her catfish relationship
jokes.punchline: Confirming she actually did mail hair to a stranger
jokes.explanation: Good callback that confirms the absurd behavior actually happened

jokes.text: This chick is douchier than Dylan. Let's get outta here!
jokes.setup: Learning about Sally's elaborate catfish revenge scheme
jokes.punchline: Dennis judging Sally while completely missing their own terrible behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis hypocrisy - judging others while being equally awful himself

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: This chick is douchier than Dylan. Let's get outta here!

jokes.text: I'm going to write 'Rude man who shushes, please call' at the top, and then, at the bottom, I'll put my phone number.
jokes.setup: Dennis planning a wanted poster for the shusher
jokes.punchline: His naive plan to put personal info on public posters
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Dennis's social media naivety coming back to bite him

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm going to write 'Rude man who shushes, please call' at the top, and then, at the bottom, I'll put my phone number.

jokes.text: I'm saying call me, let's have a phone conversation. Yeah, don't tweet. Don't just tweet me, don't shush me-- you know, let's connect.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his poster philosophy
jokes.punchline: Dennis preaching about genuine connection while obsessing over a stranger who shushed him
jokes.explanation: Dennis's hypocritical social commentary while being completely antisocial

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm saying call me, let's have a phone conversation. Yeah, don't tweet. Don't just tweet me, don't shush me-- you know, let's connect.

jokes.text: I am Dylan Toback. No, no, no... Mm-mm, mm-mm, mm-mm... Don't say that!
jokes.setup: Looking for Dylan Toback
jokes.punchline: Elderly woman claiming to be Dylan, Dennis refusing to accept reality
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection reveal with Dennis's denial of inconvenient truth

dialogue.speaker: Catfish
dialogue.line: I am Dylan Toback.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, no, no... Mm-mm, mm-mm, mm-mm... Don't say that!

jokes.text: My real name is Catfish!
jokes.setup: Elderly woman explaining her online personas
jokes.punchline: Literal name 'Catfish' while being an actual catfish
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke about catfishing with literal name reveal

dialogue.line: My real name is Catfish!

jokes.text: Don't you shush me! Don't you shush us, goddamn it. Don't disturb Jerry.
jokes.setup: Catfish shushing them near Jerry on life support
jokes.punchline: Dennis's explosive reaction to being shushed again, followed by reasonable explanation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to their shushing obsession with reasonable context making Dennis look worse

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Don't you shush me! Don't you shush us, goddamn it. Don't disturb Jerry.

jokes.text: Dee banged a fat old black lady, and then sent her hair.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining their wild goose chase to Frank
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong summary of what actually happened
jokes.explanation: Mac's completely garbled retelling shows his poor comprehension and casual prejudice

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dee banged a fat old black lady, and then sent her hair.

jokes.text: Welcome to Paddy's Pub... the oldest pub in America. Well, that's not true. Right. No, I lied. Everybody lies on the Internet.
jokes.setup: Frank making a promotional video with false claims
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission that he's lying, followed by cynical justification
jokes.explanation: Frank's shameless dishonesty presented as business strategy

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Welcome to Paddy's Pub... the oldest pub in America.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, that's not true.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Right. No, I lied.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Everybody lies on the Internet.

jokes.text: 'Cause we also got donkey shows, Motley Crue and cake.
jokes.setup: Frank listing fake amenities at Paddy's
jokes.punchline: Escalating from illegal animal shows to random band and dessert
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank chaos - random escalating promises that make no sense together

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: 'Cause we also got donkey shows, Motley Crue and cake.

jokes.text: I am a doctor... Dr. Toboggan. Mantis Toboggan.
jokes.setup: Frank claiming medical credentials in his fake video
jokes.punchline: Using his ridiculous fake doctor persona from previous episodes
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Frank's recurring fake identity, always gets a laugh from fans

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I am a doctor... Dr. Toboggan. Mantis Toboggan.

jokes.text: I said a 'viral video,' Frank. Goddamn it. Goddamn it!
jokes.setup: Learning Frank made a literal virus video
jokes.punchline: Dennis's frustrated clarification of viral vs virus too late
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the viral/virus misunderstanding with Dennis's characteristic rage

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I said a 'viral video,' Frank. Goddamn it. Goddamn it!

jokes.text: You guys open? Yes, we are. Charlie, lock the door.
jokes.setup: Customers drawn by their fake video wanting to enter Paddy's
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately trying to lock them out after getting what he wanted
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - they get exactly what they wanted but immediately reject it, classic Sunny irony

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: You guys open?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yes, we are. Charlie, lock the door.

tmdb_overview: The gang uses the Internet to cyber-stalk their prey. Frank tries to drum up business for Paddy's with a viral video.
